items.title: India: Several feared dead with many injured following stampede | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Several people are feared dead and dozens more have been injured in a crush at a massive religious festival in northern India.

Officials say a special rapid action force unit was deployed to bring the situation under control. Worshippers have been gathering for hours to take a holy dip in the river on the most significant day of Maha Kumbh Mela Festival. Authorities were expecting a record 100 million devotees to visit the site on Wednesday alone.

items.title: Danish leader feeling the heat following Trump's repeated Greenland threats | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Denmark's Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen has been racing across Europe trying to shore up support from allies, following Donald Trump's repeated threats over Greenland. The US President says he wants to acquire the semi-autonomous Danish territory, and has refused to rule out using military force to do so. His demands have sent alarm bells ringing in Copenhagen and other European capitals.

items.title: Legitimate support for M23 rebels in Goma? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In the Democratic Republic of Congo in central Africa, fighters from the M23 rebel group and their allies have entered the eastern regional capital of Goma. The Congolese government disputes M23’s claims that rebels have taken control with the city. At least 17 United Nations peacekeepers have been killed in the fighting so far. Thousands of civilians are fleeing across the border into Rwanda. The UN is warning that hospitals in Goma are overwhelmed.

items.title: Why has the Serbian Prime Minister decided to step down now? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Serbia's Prime Minister has resigned after months of demonstrations across the country.
Milos Vucevic announced he was stepping down with an appeal for calm. Cities across Serbia have seen nearly daily protests since the collapse of a train station roof in the northern city of Novi Sad in November. Fifteen people were killed. Many blame the incident on corruption and poor construction oversight. The tragedy has become a flashpoint for wider discontent with the rule of the Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic.
#serbia #vucevic

items.title: Will China's DeepSeek burst the American AI bubble? | DW News Desk
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek and its latest open-source large language model (LLM) are sending shockwaves through the world's tech industry. DeepSeek's LLM appears to match or even beat its competitors at a fraction of the cost. And DeepSeek is by far not the only AI model coming out of China. Alibaba's Qwen and Bytedance's Doubao are other examples of competitors catching up with American companies like OpenAI. US President Donald Trump says it's a 'wake-up call' for Silicon Valley, but how real is DeepSeek's AI revolution?  Can this Chinese startup truly overtake the US tech giants spending billions to win the AI race?

#NewsDesk #DeepSeek #ArtificialIntelligence

items.title: How are returning Syrians being received by the people that stayed in the country? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>EU foreign ministers meeting in Brussels have agreed on a plan to ease sanctions on Syria following the ousting of the Assad regime.

The bloc's foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said the plan would be swiftly implemented to help kickstart Syria's recovery after almost 14 years of civil war. But she said it could be reversed if the new government in Damascus does not respect human rights. Current sanctions include a ban on Syrian oil exports and financial restrictions. 

During Assad's regime, millions of Syrians left the country. Many of them are returning home for the first time in a decade. DW colleague Rama Jarmakani is one of them.

items.title: Can Germany Revive its Economy? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Germany is confronting a prolonged economic downturn, with the economy contracting for a second consecutive year. Political instability has left the nation without effective leadership, delaying essential reforms until a new government is established post-election. Despite a slight uptick in business sentiment, future expectations remain bleak. DW Business’s Daniel Winter speaks to Clemens Fuest, President of the Ifo Institute, about what needs to be done in Germany to break the cycle of economic downturn and stagnation.

items.title: Why is Rwanda supporting the M23 rebels fighting in DR Congo? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>At least 17 people have been reported killed in the Democratic Republic of Congo as rebel forces claim to have captured the eastern city of Goma.

The M23 militia has been fighting government forces and United Nations peacekeepers. The UN is warning of a humanitarian crisis. Thousands of civilians have fled across the border into Rwanda, which is accused of backing the rebels. There are reports of troops firing at each other across the frontier. Goma is home to around two million people, including many already displaced by the conflict.

items.title: How one Holocaust survivor decided to fight extremism | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The name Auschwitz has become synonymous with death, suffering and inhumanity. At least 1.1 million people were murdered there. More than 900,000 of them were Jews. 
Eighty years ago, on January 27, 1945, soldiers of the Red Army liberated Auschwitz. That day is recognized in Germany as a nationwide day of remembrance for the victims of National Socialism.  
Helene Joffe grew up in a Jewish family in France and went into hiding to escape the Nazi death camps in 1943 when she was just 8 years old. She vividly remembers the impact the Holocaust had on her family. After the war she moved to South Africa and started a new life as a teacher. DW met the 90-year-old survivor.

Chapters:
0:00 80th anniversary of Auschwitz liberation
7:50 Stephan Lehnstaedt, Holocaust and Jewish Studies Professor
13:01 Katrin Steffen, University of Sussex

items.title: Students launch 24-hour blockage in Belgrade | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>University students in Serbia's capital Belgrade launched a 24-hour blockade of a key traffic intersection on Monday, stepping up pressure on the government over the deadly collapse of a train station's roof in November that killed 15 people. Protesters blame authorities over what they feel is neglect and rampant corruption. Serbia's President Aleksandar Vucic is set later on Monday to announce "important decisions" about the situation, according to local media, without offering further detail.

items.title: 80th anniversary of Auschwitz liberation marked by survivors and world leaders | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>World leaders and Holocaust survivors gathered in the Polish town of Oswiecim to mark the 80th anniversary of the liberation of the Nazi death camp Auschwitz-Birkenau. 
The ceremony focussed on testimonies of surviving former inmates. Some wore blue and white scarves, the colors worn by the death camp prisoners. 
Several international leaders attended the event including German Chancellor Olaf Scholz, French President Emmanuel Macron and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. 
Auschwitz was the largest of the extermination camps and has become a symbol of Nazi Germany's genocide of six million Jews. Around 1.1 million people, the majority Jewish, were murdered at the site in German-occupied Poland between 1940 and 1945. 

#auschwitz #holocaust #shoah
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Eighty years after Holocaust: Jewish life in Germany still faces threats | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Today marks 80 years since the liberation of the Auschwitz death camp, recognized in Germany as a nationwide day of remembrance for the victims of the Nazi regime. But even now, Germany’s synagogues and Jewish institutions still require police protection due to antisemitic attacks and threats. The prospect of the far-right AfD performing strongly in German elections next month have only added to the sense of unease. 

00:00 Jewish communities seek to build bridges in Germany
04:20 Dalia Grinfeld, Anti-Defamation League

items.title: M23 rebels in DR Congo say they have captured Goma | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>M23 rebels in the Democratic Republic of Congo say they have captured the eastern city of Goma, after days of heavy fighting with government forces and UN peacekeepers. 
The M23 militia is backed by neighboring Rwanda, and the United Nations has warned of a major humanitarian crisis. Thousands of civilians have already fled across the border into Rwanda. There are reports that troops from the two neighbouring countries have been firing at each other across the border. Goma is the biggest city in eastern Congo - home to around 1.7 million residents and displaced people.

00:00 M23 claims capture of Goma
02:47 Journalist Al Katanty Djaffar reports from Goma
06:55 DW speaks with Myriam Favier, head of the International Red Cross's subdelegation in Goma

items.title: Israel lets Palestinians return to northern Gaza: What will they find? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Thousands of displaced Palestinians are now crossing into northern Gaza for the first time in over a year. Israel has agreed to allow their passage as part of the ceasefire deal with the militant Hamas group. 
Gazans began returning on Monday morning after waiting days to cross. Their return had been delayed after Israel blocked their passage, accusing Hamas of breaching the terms of the truce by not freeing civilian hostages first. The militants have agreed to release more Israeli captives this week in exchange.

items.title: HIV breakthrough drug priced out of reach for those who need it most | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The UN agency leading the world's response to HIV and AIDS, is making an urgent call to action: UNAIDS wants a groundbreaking HIV prevention drug to be made available to everyone who needs it. The drug, Lenacapavir, requires just two injections a year and has shown remarkable success in trials. 
But there's a problem: it currently costs each patient more than forty thousand dollars per year - that's far too expensive for most.

items.title: Has Lukashenko silenced the Belarusian opposition? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Exit polls in Belarus indicate that longtime ruler Alexander Lukashenko has won the country's presidential election. As polls close there, election authorities are already reporting that Lukashenko has captured 87-point-6 percent of the vote. Lukashenko - who has close ties to Moscow -  has now been in power since 1994. Election observers say the vote was largely orchestrated - rigged - and that any meaningful opponents are in prison or exiled.

items.title: How Arab countries are responding to Trump's 'clean out' Gaza remarks to expel Palestinians
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>On Saturday, US President Trump suggested that Palestinians be expelled from the Gaza Strip to "clean out" the territory. Trump called the Gaza Strip a "demolition site" and that ethnically cleansing Palestinians from the enclave could be a temporary or a long-term arrangement. President Trump said he had spoken to Jordanian King Abdullah about Jordan taking in Palestinians, and that he would speak to Egyptian President el-Sissi on Sunday for Egypt to do the same. Trump's "clean out" remarks have stirred controversy, as any forced expulsion of Palestinians would violate international law and jeopardize the creation of a Palestinian state.  

Chapter Breakdown: 
0:00 Palestinian Islamic Jihad announces release of Arbel Yehud
0:40 Joost Hiltermann, MENA Program Director at the International Crisis Group, on Trump's suggestion to 'clean out' Gaza and how Arab countries are responding

items.title: Palestinian Islamic Jihad announces release of Israeli civilian prior to next hostage-prisoner swap
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The militant group Palestinian Islamic Jihad in Gaza says it will free Israeli civilian hostage Arbel Yehud before the next planned swap where Israel is to release Palestinian prisoners and detainees in exchange for Hamas returning Israeli hostages. According to the terms of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire agreement, Arbel Yehud should have been handed over by Hamas this past Saturday. Hamas, however, delayed the release over what they called technical reasons. Israel has blocked tens of thousands of Palestinians from returning to their homes in northern Gaza, and says their return is conditional on Yehud's release.

DW speaks to journalist Balig Sladeen.

#arbelyehud

items.title: Can the EU compete with China’s rail plans? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A deadly accident at a train station in Serbia has put a spotlight on China’s outsized role in the Balkans and Hungary, particularly its funding for large rail projects. items.content:
<article>The Lebanese Health Ministry says at least two people are dead in southern Lebanon after Israeli soldiers fired on residents carrying Hezbollah flags.

Tensions have been rising in the area as residents have been returning to their homes despite the presence of Israeli forces. The troops were set to withdraw by Sunday under a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah militants. But Israel said on Friday they would remain there longer.

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Four female soldiers held captive in Gaza for nearly 500 days have been reunited with their families. They were released by Hamas in return for 200 Palestinian prisoners and detainees held in Israeli jails. It was the second exchange since a fragile ceasefire took effect last weekend to pause the 15-month-long war. The exchange triggered jubilation among both Israelis and Palestinians.

items.title: Israel releases 200 Palestinian prisoners and detainees | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel has freed 200 Palestinian prisoners and detainees, the second such release under a ceasefire agreement between Israel and the militant group Hamas, which came into effect last Sunday. The Red Cross is facilitating the exchange. Buses carrying some of the freed Palestinians arrived in the occupied West Bank, while others who had been serving life sentences were transferred to Egypt. 

#dwnewsshorts #palestinianprisoners #israel

items.title: Zelenskyy brushes off Putin's stated readiness for peace talks as 'manipulation' | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukraine's President Volodymr Zelenskyy has dismissed comments from Russian President Vladimir Putin that he is ready for talks to end the war in Ukraine. Zelenksyy characterized Putin's comments  as "manipulation." Putin gave an interview to state TV, praising US President Trump. He also said he and Trump had a pragmatic and trusting relationship and suggested they should meet to discuss ending the Ukraine war.

Meanwhile there are media reports that the US is halting foreign aid to almost all countries for 90 days. According to a leaked internal memo from the US State department, only two countries -  Israel and Egypt are exempt from the freeze.

#ukrainerussiawar #trump #zelenskyy
